title: Festuca chodatiana
taxonomy: Festuca chodatiana is classified within the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Poaceae and the genus Festuca. It belongs to the tribe Poeae and is a type of grass. Further classification includes it being placed within the subfamily Pooideae and the section Festuca. The species Festuca chodatiana is native to South America. Within the species classification, it is often referred to as Festuca chodatiana (Stuempfle) Stuelpnagel.

intro: Festuca chodatiana, also known as Chodat's fescue, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Festuca chodatiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support overall health and well-being, as well as to treat specific health conditions.
